free_text: Fusion probabilities in the reactions 40,48Ca + 238U
at energies around the Coulomb barrier
Advanced Science Research Center
Nishina Center for Accelerator-Based Science
Research Center for Electron Photon Science
Flerov Laboratory of Nuclear Reactions
Institut fur Kernphysik
Beam intensity is 0.5 to 1.0 particle-nA.
- Target is natural.
- Chemical-form of target: UO2
- Physical-form of target is solid.
- Target-thickness is 0.08 mg/cm2. 238U
- Backing is nickel.
- Backing-thickness is 0.09 mg/cm2. foil
with isobutane gas at a pressure 3 Torr
Proofread by K. Nishio (2013.06.28)
Received by e-mail from K.Nishio
S.Ebata
The cross sections were obtained by fitting the
fragment angular distributions as function of the
center-of-mass angle between 70 and 100 degrees to a
function given in R.Vandenbosch et al., Nuclear Fisson
(Academic Press, New York, 1973) and integrating it
over the angle.
The errors include systematic uncertainties
arising from the limited angular range
covered as well as statistical
uncertainties.
Plotted in Fig.1 of Phys. Rev. C 86, 034608
The cross sections were obtained by fitting the
fragment angular distributions as function of the
center-of-mass angle between 70 and 100 degrees to a
function given in R.Vandenbosch et al., Nuclear Fisson
(Academic Press, New York, 1973) and integrating it
over the angle.
The errors include systematic uncertainties
arising from the limited angular range
covered as well as statistical
uncertainties.
Plotted in Fig.1 of Phys. Rev. C 86, 034608
The data cover the detection angle of fission
fragments between 65 and 115 degress. The spectra are
normalized such that the total area of each spectrum
summed over the mass range is 200%.
No information on source of uncertainties
